1.texto_original: Y ya a salvo, entonces supieron que la isla se llamaba Melita.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Hechos 28:1

"Y ya a salvo, entonces supieron que la isla se llamaba Melita."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Hechos 28:1

Philological Analysis

"Y ya a salvo, entonces supieron que la isla se llamaba Melita."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Y ya a salvo, entonces supieron que la isla se llamaba Melita."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

4.texto_original: Y como los bárbaros vieron la serpiente [venenosa] colgando de su mano, se decían unos a otros: Ciertamente este hombre es homicida, a quien, escapado del mar, la justicia no deja vivir.

16.texto_original: Y cuando llegamos a Roma, el centurión entregó los presos al prefecto de la guardia, mas a Pablo le fue permitido estar aparte, con un soldado que le guardase.
16.normal:
Devotional Study of Hechos 28:16

"Y cuando llegamos a Roma, el centurión entregó los presos al prefecto de la guardia, mas a Pablo le fue permitido estar aparte, con un soldado que le guardase."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
16.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Hechos 28:16

Philological Analysis

"Y cuando llegamos a Roma, el centurión entregó los presos al prefecto de la guardia, mas a Pablo le fue permitido estar aparte, con un soldado que le guardase."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Y habiendo dicho esto, los judíos salieron, teniendo gran discusión entre sí."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

30.texto_original: Y Pablo, se quedó dos años enteros en su casa de alquiler, y recibía a todos los que a él venían,
30.normal:
Devotional Study of Hechos 28:30

"Y Pablo, se quedó dos años enteros en su casa de alquiler, y recibía a todos los que a él venían,"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
30.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Hechos 28:30

Philological Analysis

"Y Pablo, se quedó dos años enteros en su casa de alquiler, y recibía a todos los que a él venían,"

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

31.texto_original: predicando el reino de Dios y enseñando acerca del Señor Jesucristo, con toda confianza y sin impedimento.
31.normal:
Devotional Study of Hechos 28:31

"predicando el reino de Dios y enseñando acerca del Señor Jesucristo, con toda confianza y sin impedimento."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
